channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Team Liquid Release Overwatch Player Following “Anti-Black Slur”
item.content:encoded:
Team Liquid Co-CEO, Steve Arhancet, has announced the release of Rupal ‘Rupal’ Zaman from the organisation’s Overwatch team following the use of a racial slur while Liquid were scrimming against Spacestation Gaming (SSG).

Overwatch scrim incident prompts a response from Liquid Co-CEO

The player was streaming his point of view of the scrims when he sent “SHUT THE F*** UP N****” in the text chat of the game. Screenshots of the incident were quickly shared across Social Media, prompting a statement from Arhancet.

Arhancet announced the release on X, stating that he was “unequivocal in condemning the language he [Rupal] used.” Arhancet added, “After a thorough review, we have decided to release Rupal and remove him from our Overwatch roster.”

“When we bring a player into Team Liquid, our expectation is that they represent themselves and us at the highest levels of professionalism,” said the Co-CEO, detailing the media training Liquid players are given before adding, “We believe in that process and hold everyone who represents Team Liquid to that same standard. But today it was not enough, and we take that seriously.”

The Liquid Co-Owner added, “We hope this becomes a moment of genuine reflection and growth for him. Not the end of his story, but a turning point in it.”

Rupal responds to Liquid release

Following the announcement, the player posted the following statement on X:

“No excuses from me. I was raised in a Bengali household in America. I myself have experienced racism myself not only in America but in other countries as well. Therefore, I should know that using such offensive language is terrible and not something that should be said so casually. It’s a horrendous mistake on my part.”

Adding, “I don’t consider myself a hateful person, and always enjoy meeting new people regardless of their background. Sorry to all who supported me. I take responsibility for my mistakes and will go on a break to reflect and work on myself.”

Rupal joined Liquid in January 2026, claiming an Overwatch Champions Series 2025 NA Stage 3 victory over Spacestation Gaming in October before a 7th-8th place finish in the OWCS World Finals in November.

You can discuss the incident and more on Insider Gaming’s Discord.

For more Overwatch news, see the February player peak here.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Today’s Contexto: Hints & Answer For March 17, 2026.
item.content:encoded:
Contexto is a word association game that gives you a new word to guess every single day. With every attempt, the AI algorithm determines how close your guess is to the daily answer based on thematic and contextual similarity.

Sometimes, though, no matter how experienced you are, the word you’re looking for can be incredibly vague and hard to deduce. Luckily for you, this guide is full of hints to give you a helping hand, but make sure you give today’s Contexto a go if you haven’t already!

How To Play Contexto

Contexto is incredibly simple to play, but difficult to master. All you have to do is type words into the search bar, and the AI algorithm will tell you how close your word is to the answer.

You’ll know how close your guess is based on the colour of the bar and the number next to it.

  • Red includes guesses with the number 1,501 and above, meaning you’re way off from the secret word.
  • Orange includes guesses between 301 and 1,500, meaning you’re getting closer, but still some ways off.
  • Green includes guesses between 1 and 300, meaning you’re on the right track.

Best Contexto Tips & Tricks

  • Start off using broad terms, then narrow down once you’ve got under the 1,000 mark.
  • Try using Nouns first, and dip into verbs or adjectives once you’ve discovered the context.
  • Experiment with random words at times, especially as different forms of a root word can rank differently, such as work and worker.
  • Guess away, as unlike other word games like Wordle, you aren’t limited to a strict number of attempts.

Best Starting Words For Contexto

Here are some great starting words to get you going with Contexto:

  • Thing
  • Person
  • Place
  • Animal
  • Work
  • Nature
  • Game
  • Concept
  • Idea
  • Food

Contexto Hints For Today, March 17, 2026

Without giving away today’s Contexto answer, here are a few helpful hints to get you thinking in the right direction:

  • Hint 1: Bird
  • Hint 2: Distinctive honk
  • Hint 3: Silly _____

If they don’t help, then here are the nine closest words, going from tenth to second:

  • Mallard
  • Chicken
  • Waterfowl
  • Pheasant
  • Deer
  • Moose
  • Bird
  • Turkey
  • Duck

Today’s Contexto Answer For March 17, 2026

If you’ve tried everything and still can’t figure out today’s Contexto answer, then you can find it down below:

Answer: Goose

A Goose is a large waterbird with a long neck, short legs, and a short, broad bill. They’re generally larger than ducks, and are often used to describe a silly person.

When Is The Next Contexto Game?

Contexto is updated daily at 12 AM or Midnight in your respective time zone, meaning the next game will start on March 18.

The Contexto schedule is very consistent, with a new game releasing at the same time every day.

How did you find today’s Contexto? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more word game guides, check out the following:

  • NYT Connections

MOBILE APP

Available on iOS & Android
